steps.title: Identify the causal connection
steps.content: The first sentence establishes Leda's orbital direction; the second sentence names the term for that direction. The blank must show that this terminology is a consequence of the orbital direction.

steps.title: Select the consequence transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the two sentences is cause-effect. Because Leda orbits in the same direction that Jupiter rotates, it receives the label "prograde" — that is the direct consequence of its orbital direction. The blank needs a transition signaling that consequence.
  • Choice A — "In reality,": signals a correction — the second sentence would contradict a mistaken assumption. No assumption is being corrected here; the second sentence simply names the result. ✗
  • Choice B — "Nevertheless,": signals contrast despite an obstacle. Receiving the label "prograde" is not a surprising or contrary outcome — it follows logically from the orbital direction. ✗
  • Choice C — "Hence,": signals that the second sentence is a logical result of the first, fitting the cause-effect relationship perfectly. ✓
  • Choice D — "Next,": signals the next step in a time sequence. The passage is not describing a sequence of events; it is explaining a logical consequence. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
